name: Lekki Muslim Ummah Group of Schools
description: Islamic group of schools (nursery to secondary, day and boarding) founded in 2016; integrates Qur'anic teachings with a British-Nigerian curriculum, aiming at moral and academic excellence.
address: 81A, Hakeem Dickson Street, Lekki Phase 1, Lagos
evidence: NigeriaPrivateSchools directory profile lists it as a government-approved Islamic day/boarding school at 81A Hakeem Dickson Street, Lekki Phase 1, with phone and email.

name: Lekki Qur'an Memorisation College
description: Tahfiz (Qur'an memorisation) college founded in October 2016 within the Lekki Muslim Ummah group, designed so students attain Islamic and secular education simultaneously.
evidence: Official Lekki Muslim Ummah Group of Schools site describes the college, founded October 2016, for memorisation of the Holy Qur'an alongside secular education.

name: LEMU Institute of Arabic and Islamic Studies
description: Arabic and Islamic studies institute (madrasah) of the Lekki Muslim Ummah, established in 2012 and serving the local and surrounding communities.
evidence: Official group website lists the institute (established 2012) among its schools for Arabic and Islamic studies.

name: Imaad Schools Lekki
description: Muslim British international secondary school (day and boarding) with British-Nigerian curriculum and Islamic faith inclination; motto 'Upholding Values for Life'.
address: Plot 1-6, Imaad Close, by Northern Foreshore Estate, off Agungi-Ajiran Road, Agungi Bus Stop, Lekki, Lagos
evidence: SchoolsCompass profile lists it as an Islam-inclined day/boarding secondary school at Imaad Close, Agungi, Lekki, with website www.imaadedu.com.

name: At-Taleem Islamic School
description: Not-for-profit Islamic nursery and primary school founded to provide a structured platform for communicating the beliefs, values and traditions of Islam through modern, experiential learning.
address: 21, Sikiru Alade Oloko Street, Lekki Phase 1, Lagos
evidence: Babymigo listing gives the school's street address in Lekki Phase 1 and describes its Islamic education focus (also rated on Edusko as an Islamic nursery/primary school in Lekki).

name: Tender Sprouts School
description: Muslim school in Ajah weaving Islamic values into education, with academics, Qur'anic studies and small class sizes for creative hands-on learning.
address: 3 & 5, Yemi Suara Street, Graceland Estate, Ajiwe, Lekki-Epe Expressway, Eti-Osa, Lagos
evidence: Official website titled 'Muslim school in Ajah, Lagos' gives full address in Ajiwe/Ajah, phone and email.

name: Al Fitrah Academy
description: Islamic secondary academy in Awoyaya described as 'an Islamic and circular educational academy, where we teach children to be beneficial to the society'; day school on the Nigerian curriculum.
evidence: Edusko school profile lists Al Fitrah Academy Awoyaya as an Islamic educational academy (secondary, day) in Awoyaya, Lagos State.

name: Guided Treasures Children's School
description: Co-educational Islam-inclined school (creche, preschool, elementary) combining Islamic and secular education using Nigerian, Cambridge and Islamic curricula.
address: 2, Prophet Olanrewaju Street, off Agboola Kolawole Street, across Gbetu New Road, before MayFair Gardens, Awoyaya
evidence: SchoolsCompass profile states Islam faith inclination, gives the Awoyaya street address and website.

name: Al Ihssan College, Osoroko
address: Osoroko, Ibeju-Lekki
evidence: Listed as 'AL IHSSAN COLLEGE OSOROKO' in a compilation of Lagos secondary school names and addresses; Islamic (Al-Ihsan) name.

name: At-Tezkiyah Islamic School, Ibeju-Agbe
address: Ibeju-Agbe, Ibeju-Lekki
evidence: Listed as 'AT-TEZKIYAH ISLAMIC SCHOOL IBEJU-AGBE' in a compilation of Lagos secondary school names and addresses.

name: Al-Minaaj Schools, Epe
description: Islamic-oriented school providing quality Islamic and Western education, committed to 'building a legacy of learning and success' through blended traditional and innovative approaches.
address: 1, Old Odoshiwola Road, Opposite Ora Estate, Mojoda, Epe, Lagos State
evidence: Official website states it is an Islamic-focused school and gives full Mojoda, Epe address, phones and email.

name: Crescent Schools, Victoria Island
description: Islam-inclined co-educational school (EYFS, primary and secondary) founded 4 October 1981 by the Muslim Ladies' Circle; Qur'an, Arabic and Islamic Studies feature across sections on a British-Nigerian curriculum.
address: 1004 Housing Estate, Opposite Civic Centre, Victoria Island, Lagos
evidence: SchoolsCompass profile lists Islamic faith inclination and the 1004 Housing Estate, Victoria Island address with website.

name: Madrasatu Khadeejata binti Khuwaylid
description: Provides Islamic and secular education, integrating Islamic, Saudi, British and Nigerian curricula.
address: Oshodi Street, Dolphin Estate, Ikoyi, Lagos
evidence: Raising Mumeens Muslim-schools directory lists the madrasah at Dolphin Estate, Ikoyi with an Islamic-plus-secular curriculum description.

name: The Outliers School
description: Muslim school in Surulere (preschool through secondary) blending British, Nigerian and Islamic curricula, emphasising academic excellence, character, soft skills and emotional intelligence.
address: 59 Bode Thomas Street, Surulere, Lagos
evidence: Official website ('The Best Muslim School In Surulere Lagos') gives address on Bode Thomas Street, phones and email.

name: Alhaleem International Schools
description: Islamic-oriented private institution (creche through secondary) founded 3 June 1991, offering unified learning on British/Nigerian/Islamic curricula to train students distinguished in character and learning.
address: 21 & 34 Erelu Danisa Street, Ijeshatedo, Surulere; 17, 19 & 21 Olumegbon Street, off Funsho Williams Avenue, Surulere, Lagos
evidence: Official website describes the Islamic-oriented school and lists both Surulere campus addresses and phone.

name: Al-Furqaan Schools Lagos
description: Islamic-centred institution (creche, nursery, primary and secondary) integrating quality secular education with sound Islamic teachings, blending the Nigerian curriculum with Qur'anic and Arabic studies.
address: 40 Salawu Street, Ojuelegba, Surulere, Lagos
evidence: NigeriaPrivateSchools profile describes the Islamic-centred creche-to-secondary school at Ojuelegba, Surulere with phone, email and website.

name: Ansar Ud Deen Academy, Surulere
address: 5, Owolewa Street, off Randle Avenue, Surulere, Lagos
evidence: Lagos OEQA approved private secondary schools list (approved 2007) shows Ansar Ud Deen Academy at 5 Owolewa Street, off Randle Avenue, Surulere; Ansar-Ud-Deen is a Muslim educational society.

name: Ansar-Ud-Deen Grammar School, Surulere
description: Founded in 1958 by the Ansar-Ud-Deen Society; day and boarding school encouraging academic and technical excellence.
evidence: Named in NigeriaPrivateSchools' 'Top Islamic Private Schools in Lagos' article as an Islamic school in Surulere founded 1958.

name: Ansar-Ud-Deen Girls' High School, Itire
description: Described as one of the oldest secondary schools in Lagos, providing qualitative education with high moral and spiritual values.
evidence: Named in NigeriaPrivateSchools' 'Top Islamic Private Schools in Lagos' article as an Ansar-Ud-Deen (Muslim society) girls' school in Itire.

name: Al Mufeed Knowledgeable Secondary School
address: 53 Ondo Street, Ebute Metta, Lagos
evidence: Listed as 'AL MUFEED KNOWLEDGEABLE SECONDARY SCHOOL 53 ONDO STREET EBUTE METTA' in a Lagos secondary schools compilation; Arabic/Islamic name (Al-Mufeed).

name: Ansar-Ud-Deen Nursery and Primary School, Ebute Metta
description: Islamic nursery and primary school named among Islamic institutions serving the Ebute Metta area.
evidence: Nigeria Property Centre's Ebute Metta East area guide names it among Islamic institutions in the area.

name: Ahlussunnah Mosque and Islamic School, Ebute Metta
description: Islamic school attached to the Ahlussunnah Mosque, named among Islamic institutions serving the Ebute Metta area.
evidence: Nigeria Property Centre's Ebute Metta East area guide names 'Ahlussunnah Mosque and Islamic School' among Islamic institutions in the area.

name: AAMCO Schools, Apapa
address: Cemetery Road, Trinity Bus-Stop, Araromi, Apapa, Lagos
evidence: Listed under Finelib's 'Arabic Schools in Nigeria' (Lagos) category with an Apapa street address.

name: Iman Comprehensive College
description: Muslim community co-educational day and boarding secondary school established in 2005; motto 'Knowledge Enriched With Faith', with Qur'an memorisation classes, science labs, ICT and separate male/female hostels.
address: Iman College Bus Stop (Mopol Junction), Ago Okota Palace Road, along Apple Junction, Amuwo Odofin, Lagos
evidence: Official website describes a Muslim community day/boarding secondary school in Amuwo Odofin; contact page gives full address, phones and emails.

name: Thaqafatu Islamiyyah College
address: 1/3, Thaqafatul Islamiyyah Street, Mebamu New Layout (Nica Bus Stop), Badagry Expressway, Lagos
evidence: Lagos OEQA 2020-2023 approved private secondary schools list (approved 2020) shows Thaqafatu Islamiyyah College at Thaqafatul Islamiyyah Street, Mebamu New Layout, Badagry Expressway.

name: Barakat Islamic Model School, Ajegunle
evidence: Listed as 'BARAKAT ISLAMIC MODEL SCHOOL AJEGUNLE' in a Lagos secondary schools compilation; no street address given, and which Ajegunle (Apapa vs Ikorodu Road) is not specified.

name: Ansar-Ud-Deen Society Senior Grammar School, Badagry
description: Senior secondary school established 1 September 1980, aiming 'to provide excellent education for self reliance and social reorientation'; students sit WAEC and NECO.
address: 73, Samuel Ekundayo Street, Badagry, Lagos
evidence: Nigerian Schools Directory (Erudites) listing gives the Ansar-Ud-Deen Society school's Badagry street address and establishment date.

name: Ansar-Ud-Deen Society Junior Grammar School, Badagry
description: Co-educational junior secondary school established 6 January 2003, providing 'high quality education accessible to all learners'.
address: 73, Samuel Ekundayo Street, Badagry, Lagos
evidence: Nigerian Schools Directory (Erudites) listing gives the Ansar-Ud-Deen Society junior school's Badagry street address and establishment date.
